verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a canonical cytosolic chaperone with no membrane anchor at baseline. However, it has multiple well-documented non-baseline surface pools: (1) cancer-cell ecto-display, a hallmark of transformation recognized by surface-directed CAR-T and antibody programs; (2) apoptosis/necroptosis-induced PM translocation; (3) stress-induced surface exposure under acute cellular stress. These context-dependent surface presentations are extensively characterized by flow cytometry, surface biotinylation, and clinical immunotherapy. The protein qualifies as contextual via cell_state_induced, not no, because surface accessibility is a defined non-baseline feature of transformed and dying cells.
